source_note: Keeper Rulebook chase procedure assigns movement actions from adjusted MOV relative to the slowest participant, and active chase hazards, barriers, and conflicts resolve inside chase action economy rather than by pushed rerolls.

speed_roll.source_note: Keeper Rulebook p132: each participant makes a CON roll (on foot) or Drive Auto roll (vehicle) to adjust MOV for the whole chase.

escape_criterion.source_note: Keeper Rulebook p132: the chase is not played out if the fleeing adjusted MOV exceeds the pursuer's adjusted MOV.

starting_range.source_note: Keeper Rulebook p133: default starting gap is 2 locations; 1 for a tenser chase; not advised beyond 2.

vehicles.source_note: Keeper Rulebook Table V (pp.145-146): common chase vehicles with MOV, Build, armor, and passenger capacity. MOV here is the chase MOV (1 location/round baseline for the slowest). Build is the vehicle's structural Build (used for collision damage and ramming). Armor reduces incoming collision/weapon damage. Passengers is the maximum including the driver.

car_economy.label: Economy car (e.g. Model T, compact sedan)

vehicular_collisions.source_note: Keeper Rulebook Table VI (p.147): collision severity tiers by narrative outcome. Each tier applies Build damage to the vehicle(s) and an HP cost to passengers. Passenger damage assumes an un-fastened occupant; a successful Drive Auto or CON-based roll may halve it at the Keeper's discretion.

minor.label: Minor (fender-bender, low-speed bump)
minor.description: Low-speed bump; vehicle may be drivable. Passengers are jolted.

moderate.label: Moderate (side-swipe, clipped obstacle)
moderate.description: Significant impact; vehicle impaired but possibly moving. Passengers thrown about.

severe.label: Severe (head-on at speed, roll-over)
severe.description: Major structural damage; vehicle likely disabled. Passengers at risk of major wounds.

mayhem.label: Mayhem (high-speed crash, multi-vehicle)
mayhem.description: Catastrophic wreck; vehicle destroyed. Survival unlikely without restraint/luck.

roadkill.label: Roadkill (pedestrian / cyclist struck at speed)
roadkill.description: Vehicle strikes an unprotected person; the struck party suffers the bulk of the damage.
